back_translation: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
'If one ventures to speak a word with you, will you be weary? Yet who can hold back from speaking?
Behold, you have instructed many, and you have strengthened feeble hands.
Your words have steadied those who stumbled, and you have strengthened buckling knees.
But now trouble has come to you, and you are weary; it strikes you, and you are dismayed.
Is not your fear of God your confidence, and the uprightness of your ways your hope?
Consider now: who that was innocent ever perished? And where were the upright destroyed?
As I have seen, those who plow iniquity and sow trouble reap the same.
They perish by the breath of God, and are consumed by the wind of His anger.
The roar of the lion and the voice of the fierce lion—even the teeth of the young lions are broken.
The old lion perishes for lack of prey, and the cubs of the lioness are scattered.
A word came to me secretly, and my ear caught only a whisper of it.
Amid troubling thoughts from visions of the night, when deep sleep falls on men,
fear and trembling seized me and made all my bones shudder.
Then a spirit glided past my face, and the hair of my body bristled.
It stood still, but I could not discern its appearance; a form was before my eyes, and I heard a quiet voice saying:
'Can a mortal be more righteous than God? Can a man be more pure than his Maker?
Behold, even His servants God does not trust, and He charges His angels with error;
how much more those who dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed as easily as a moth!
They are broken from morning to evening; unnoticed, they perish forever.
Are not their tent cords pulled up? They die, and that without wisdom.'
